page: Amazon Web Services c5a.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. The instance provides 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, delivering dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware, and the baseline network bandwidth is 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate moderate performance in specific single-threaded tasks but lower efficiency in multi-core and memory-intensive operations. The server achieves average ratings in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench text processing. However, multi-core performance in stress-ng and bzip3 decompression falls into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests and Passmark memory scores also rank in the lower tiers, reflecting a performance tradeoff for memory-bound workloads.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC cores and compute-focused 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the c5a.xlarge offers an entry-level compute-optimized profile. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and single-threaded compression tasks. However, it is less suited for memory-intensive databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 1 Gbps baseline network connection. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities in single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing, but reveal limitations in multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, where scores fall into the lower percentiles. This hardware profile is best suited for lightweight compute workloads, basic web serving, and single-threaded data processing, offering dedicated CPU resource allocation without the overhead of larger memory footprints.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance with a balanced single-threaded profile for lightweight processing and basic web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor at 3.3 GHz
8.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Average performance in single-threaded compression and text processing
Best suited for lightweight web serving and basic compute tasks
